reviews.text: Lloyd and his team are very good, communicative and friendly! They made sure to explain the process and my options the entire way. He held himself with so much confidence and I could tell right away how knowledgeable and experienced he was in my area of charges I was facing.   Even while working on multiple cases, he still made it a point to send me a quick email, provide an update, etc. Not only did he help get my exaggerated charges completely dropped, Kayla, at his firm chose to assist me in my next challenge, finalizing my divorce. Kayla and Lloyd are both personable and really go the extra mile for their clients. If someone came to me looking for a lawyer, I would absolutely without hesitation recommend Lloyd and Kayla at Suhr Law!

reviews.text: Nice people but not a fan. Communications poor and not timely, ex: requested a discussion ref pre trail three weeks prior but discussion didn’t occur until day prior. Over all I didn’t feel a ton of support.
reviews.ownerResponse:
While we appreciate any and all feedback to better serve our clients, I, as the owner of the firm, wanted to take a moment and provide a brief response to your review.

It appears your biggest concern was with regard to communication. I can assure you that our staff and attorneys work very hard every day to make sure that each client is fully and timely informed about the circumstances and options in their case. I can even recall assisting you personally back on April 8th when you had stopped in to pick up a document and you had questions.

Regarding the pretrial discussion you referenced, our office did not receive a settlement offer from the prosecution until the day before your pretrial conference hearing (notwithstanding repeated requests for one sooner). This was frustrating to us as well, but we could not have the discussion you were looking for until we had that settlement offer in hand and we do not have control over when the offer is provided. Once we got the offer we immediately contacted you to discuss it and the options it presented.

We did appreciate and support you as a client, which is why we voluntarily waived a portion of your fee that remained unpaid and we also assisted you free of charge on an unrelated matter that you had contacted us about (and which we got dismissed for you).

Again, while we appreciate all feedback, I felt it necessary to respond on and with these points. We wish you well going forward.

While it is regrettable that you feel this way, and our many other positive reviews reflect a very different experience than what you have described, personality differences and individualized opinions as to how the legal system should work (versus how it actually functions) simply do not make every professional working relationship sustainable or realistic. reviews.text:
Absolute horrific experience, I have called this law firm several times about some litigation work. I spoke with a lawyer once , a nice paralegal who all were helpful. I was waiting for a call back. The call didn't come so I called

I got Sunny, she was nice but when she looked up my record she became apprehensive and made it seem like I couldn't have the service I was calling the office and wanting to pay for. But I spoke with someone higher up, and at the same level before, they had no issues. So I didn't really care but Sunny wasn't helping me on the phone call until she could get " my record looked at" so on so forth.

I politely tell her my past experience,who I spoke with in the office. I'm not worried about that, I would appreciate it she would stop steering the convo in that direction. Unneeded stress.

This criticism seemed to provoke Sunny to mention the process more become even more unhelpful ,refusing to defuse. I asked to be transfered she refused , seemingly wanting to exasperate the situation instead of diffusing.

Maybe Sunny is used to provoking people when receiving criticism  but I do not appreciate it during my stressful day, and I know most people are above that class in there right mind as adults

So I call back after 30 minutes and say hello, I don't get one in return.

There is a loud angry male ( identified as one of the lawyers)screaming at me  immediately, doesn't give me a chance to talk.  Immediately threatens me with police I try to call his publicly listed business again. I am beyond confused.

As a manager is customer service I'm well aware there is no circumstances to life that allow you to correctly respond with so much pure hostility..........Unless having the precious circumstances at the beginning of life that allow to be lawyer also allows you speak to people however which way you please......no it doesn't.

I'm not sure what personal relationship Sunny and this lawyer might have that would cause such a emotional over reaction, but I wouldn't get in the middle of them. They are above criticism.

I however do not care and will politely explain, you should coach your staff more efficient at gathering information, focus less on being affected after receiving a clear criticism.

You are a law office the victims are calling the phone, not slightly offended because of a petty conversation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Sparks:

Our staff and attorneys work very hard to accommodate the needs of prospective and current clients, as shown by our many other very positive reviews. Unfortunately you became increasingly argumentative during the phone conversation with our receptionist and when it became obvious that the conversation was not going to be productive, it was terminated. When you repeatedly called back after being informed we would be unable to assist, it was necessary to inform you that continuing calls would result in law enforcement being contacted. We hope you are able to find someone with whom there is a better fit for your circumstances.

reviews.text: I got my 5th DUI in 15 years. I was looking at prison time, loosing my house, my trucks, my job, my dogs and my cat. I looked into several law firms for legal representation. Most wanted $7,000 to $15,000 retainers and every one of them told me to start looking for new homes for my pets except for Chris. He only wanted a fraction of the cost of other lawyers and told me exactly what I wanted to hear. I didn’t give him much to work with but in the end he found a way to drastically reduce the consequences for my actions. He found a treatment program that allowed me to stay out of prison and keep everything I’ve worked so hard to get over the years. He was worth every penny. I would have paid him double what he charged and even then he would have still been cheaper than all the other lawyers that told me to start making arrangements for prison. Hopefully I never need legal representation again but they will definitely be my first call if I do.
Chris did a great job working on my case he got me a great deal and got my charge reduced. I'd definitely recommend him to anybody

He constantly demands his payments on time for nothing. I don't even know how he gets his professional license approved. It's more about making money than representing anyone. I do not recommend using this law firm unless you want your case to drag out for months and like not having your emails or phone calls returned. This whole process has been ridiculous. I am more than disgusted with their services. I am still trying to get my divorce finalized and haven't heard from her in 3 weeks. No response to my emails or the messages I've left with her office.

One star is too generous.

Do not retain Carey. She consistently filed documents late and missed deadlines, which caused important information not to be obtained. I had to follow up constantly because she was disorganized and forgetful — even after phone calls, she wouldn’t remember what was discussed. I eventually told her and her assistant that I would not keep repeating myself; Carey simply needed to do her job.

When I raised concerns about her poor performance, she went completely silent. Then, she had the audacity to send me a bill for over $3,000 — despite doing almost nothing on my case. I requested a detailed billing statement several times and was ignored.

She often blamed her lack of follow-through on medical issues. If that’s the case, she shouldn’t be taking on active cases. Most of her time on calls was spent gossiping about opposing counsel instead of focusing on my case.

An absolutely unprofessional experience from start to finish.

By Monday I had sent her my proof of financing and other contact information requested. By Monday evening she informed me that I was not invited to their office for public bidding with no specific reason given. The Wiggens family and the North Dakota State School of Science, who are the sellers of this land, are getting cheated out of the receiving fair price for this property. I was not given a fair opportunity to bid by Smith, Strege and Fredericksen Law Firm probably on account of them being legal counsel for a tenant who would not repair extensive damage to one of my properties. The case was settled out of court towards my favor which is reason to believe why I got disqualified from bidding at this land sale. Any family or party who puts this law firm in charge of selling their land will not receive the true market value of their land.

reviews.text: I used Lommen Law Firm for the creation of a custom NFA firearm trust, and I am extremely pleased with the exceptional service and expertise Brandon provided. My experience  with Brandon was amazing. I started a lawsuit (personal injury) in 2019 with a different law firm. That law firm failed to file with the court system and it was completely dismissed.

Brandon was then hired as attorney of record to fix the mistake and finish the case on my behalf.  in 2021 Brandon was able to get the court system to reaccept my case with a judge saying my previous lawyers were at fault and I should not be penalized on their behalf. First time since 1980s this style of ruling was forced into the court system in Minnesota.

After that Brandon went for the gold getting over a 7 figure settlement in my case. Polk county Minnesota does not award large amount and Brandon was able to break though that threshold.

After my case Brandon went with me to meet with financial planners to ensure I would have a funds set aside for future medical expenses and life events. He was able to help guide the conversation because I had never talked about investing close to half a million dollars with anyone before. I was unsure what conversations and questions to ask.

Thank you Brandon for your hard work.

reviews.text:
I hired John Arechigo as I was facing a C Felony terrorizing with a dangerous weapon and an A misdemeanor concealed weapons charge in North Dakota.  This being the 1st time I have ever been in trouble I didn't know how the outcome was going to be or how the process worked.  I was facing a minimum 2 year prison sentence for the felony with a maximum of 5 years.  I was also sitting in jail on a $500,000 bond.

The 1st conversation with John I felt like I was in good hands, he was very upfront, honest, and willing to help.  As the process went along he was extremely helpful and very good at explaining the processes and what happens step by step as I was new to all of this.  John never sugar coated anything and did not make promises of false hope.  John was very easy to talk with, very realistic, and very hardworking - exceptionally well prepared for everything.

John made me feel that I was always a priority as I knew he was busy with other cases as well.  John was always available when I needed to get in touch about my case.

After 5 months of hearings, motions, and trial prep John got positive results for me without going to trial:

1st, John was able to get the misdemeanor concealed weapons charged dismissed by the judge.

2nd, John was able to get the felony terrorizing with a dangerous weapon charge reduced to a misdemeanor terrorizing charge with the dangerous weapon clause dropped from the charge.

My punishment was 31 days time served, 329 days suspended, 1 year probation which included (24/7 program and no firearms for the 1 year) and a $2000 fine.

Overall I feel the outcome was better than I hoped for.  I would highly recommend John!

Toby
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for the kind words, Toby. We value the trust you placed in deciding to hire John for your case. We're glad we were able to secure a good outcome for you. Best of luck to you!

reviews.text: I’ve always looked at reviews before I decided to make a decision about whatever it was that I was doing. He told me in the consultation that I will get what I pay for if I chose to retain him.  And he couldn’t have been more honest when he said that. He completely took a drastic situation where It was definitely a great possibility that I would be looking at prison time and turned it into the best possible outcome that I could’ve never imagined was possible for the situation at hand. I had a somewhat high profile drug case and John handled my case with care and respect and used his very high level of education and experience to get me the best outcome that neither I or my family could’ve imagined. reviews.text:
I called and left a message.

Was called back by someone who wasn't an attorney to be told that they didn't want to try to help me find an avenue to see my daughter.

I so understand my situation is hard but to be told we don't even think it's worth have an attorney call you back who is able to give you insight into other directions you may be able to go. Is simply wrong.

I know this to be the case because when I asked for her name again to make sure I have my facts right.. she hung up.

I am sure they help other people but they wouldn't even try to help me. I don't know if I would want that kind of person on my side. They may help you win, but the question is at what cost. Not just money wise but all the things that actually matter, like how it will hurt your kids, or your ability to co-parent.

So I have no problem with you spinning what happened how you want. Its not right to be less than truthfully. However, it could be argued that there are always three versions of the truth. My version, your version and what really happened.

However, to put out information that I didn't offer and isn't readily available anywhere.. is simply unacceptable and unethical fix it or I will make a complaint.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Unfortunately the issue you described to our staff regarding your adult daughter was not something for which we felt legal action could remedy.

You were offered the opportunity to schedule a consultation with one of our lawyers, but you declined upon being informed the consultation would not be free of charge.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Outstanding Attorney — Professional, Honest, and Truly in Your Corner

I can’t recommend Steven Fischer highly enough. He has guided me through extremely complex and stressful legal situations, and I genuinely consider him my lifetime attorney.

From the very beginning, he demonstrated a deep understanding of the law and the realities of the courtroom. He doesn’t tell you what you want to hear — he tells you the truth, and that honesty is invaluable. He clearly explains the process, lays out realistic outcomes, and helps you make informed decisions with confidence.

What truly sets him apart is how he goes to bat for you not only in court, but throughout the waiting process as well — the part of a case that can be the most stressful and uncertain. He actively advocates for you during that time, managing pressure, pushing where appropriate, and helping move cases toward resolution and finality. That level of involvement makes a real difference.

He doesn’t just defend you legally — he helps carry the weight of the situation so it doesn’t consume your life. Knowing he was handling everything properly allowed me to focus on my responsibilities while he stayed on top of the case. That peace of mind is priceless.

He is strategic, responsive, professional, and prepared. You can tell he knows his craft inside and out, and he treats you like a person, not just a case file.

If you ever find yourself in a jam and need someone who will stand with you from start to finish — someone who understands the realities, tells you the truth, and truly has your back — this is the attorney you want. I’m extremely grateful for his work and wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him to anyone in need of strong, reliable legal representation.

He was very helpful and friendly with the questions I needed answers to. I am very sad to say I called for help and didn't receive the kind I expected... it was quite the opposite of legal, I'm not legal Swavay and I asked for, what I thought was what I  needed. A consultation for an employment issue & also how much it would be. His response was that he's from the fargo area is that what/ where I'm looking for? I replied that i was calling for technically 2 reasons if he could help with both the other which will need to be settled in Fargo. Just the matter i wanted to handle now was my employment issue and he then asked me to explain my situation. Now I'll mention again I'm not very smart with ...well anything. I told him how 1 of my managers was being unfair and I needed to know my rights, and like what to do, or who to call (like I said not the brightest). He asked me to just explain to him what happened and so I began from the beginning a bit saying how I've I've there for 5 days explain the main issue that led to the incident with my manager. And it may have taken maybe 2 mins to get to where I was, in which the next several words out of my mouth would have been the conflict with my superior.. it was apparently 2 mins to long cause he interruped me saying "OK!' in such a unexpected, unnecessaryand unprofessional tone, along with 'So what is it you need help with?! "I had a lump in my throat and told him just forget it and hung up. As I'm sure it's noticeable but, I don't know how to sum things up and get my point across<^>.... I'm sorry I was born this way , I try not to... I thought that I actually had some sense of hope for once with something like this sorry for not understanding and calling the wrong person for the wrong reason. This is what happens when i try! thanks for humbling me.
If could put a negative star on here i would! very rude, showed he really didnt want t help. then completely offered an amount that was outrageous
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for calling me. I am very sorry to hear that you felt I was being rude.  Some callers are unhappy when I disagree with their opinion of the law as it applies to them.  Perhaps that is what happened here.  I try very hard to suggest a fair fee based on the amount and type of work that a caller is requesting. I rely on my more than 35 years of helping people with their legal problems to provide an accurate estimate of the expected legal fees.  In situations that are complex and may require a lot of time, my fee can seem expensive. I always want a caller to know how much I estimate their legal fees will be before we start a project.  I do not want there to be unexpected fees for the project. I try to make my fees as transparent as possible.  Now that you know what I think it will cost to complete the work you were requesting you can discuss your situation with other attorneys and hopefully you will be able to find an attorney that can help you for what you consider a reasonable fee.

reviews.text: This firm was recommended to me personally, Angela Meyer. I was sued. I have never needed an attorney and I have not ever had to wade through the legal system. Once Grant took over representing me for a small claim suit, I was able to calmly wait for the court date. Grant gave me confidence to face a situation that brought on many emotions, and I was able to deal with it logically and based in facts with his guidance. Even if I had been judged against, due to my ignorance, I still believe Grant did the work that needed to be done, he told me the truth, and he put my mind at ease. I recommend Grant and this law firm. Many people told me I did not need an attorney. The facts were clear. One person told me I should hire an attorney so that I could remove anxiety that law suits and accusations cause. I now fully agree with the one person. Money well spent. Thank you. I now know anyone can bring a law suit with or without cause. I hope that I never need Grant's help again, but I will call him again, if that time should ever arise.

reviews.text: Best attorneys in the area. Treat you like a human & know the law. If there’s a way they will find it . This whole law firm has did more than I can ask for & some. For my family & myself . You want a good attorney your at the right place.

reviews.text: Top notch service. Caring, kind, compassionate, empathetic. All the qualities you need in family law, while still ensuring the best possible outcome. Highly recommend!

Colin Hennessy recently handled my divorce and custody case, and I could not be more pleased with the work he did as well as Travis Jung, the other attorney and Jessica, the Paralegal assigned to my case. First and foremost, in saying they're a law firm for families, it is not just a tagline. From day one, it was clear that their first priority was the wellbeing of my children. Their primary concern was what is in the children's best interest and that came through loud and clear in every single conversation and decision made. We had conversations about their wellbeing and mine throughout the course of the divorce and they weren't surface level; they were out of genuine care. There were factors that were out of the ordinary for my divorce and made it challenging for me. The team at Gjesdahl made sure I always felt cared for and treated the situation with compassion and grace. They involved me in decisions about strategy, walked beside me through the process, and were steadfast in making sure I felt comfortable. They were honest and caring when delivering news that was hard to hear but helped me understand what I could do along with them to ensure the best possible outcome (which we achieved, by the way). They were good stewards of my money too, making sure I understood what this would cost, what alternate paths would cost (and the likely outcome), and making decisions on how to do work based on expense as well as potential success. You sometimes hear horror stories of legal cases where people feel billable hours were artificially inflated but I feel the opposite here - they were very mindful of the expense.

There are not enough words to express the gratitude I feel for the team at Gjesdahl Law. During one of the most challenging times of your life, it's a gift to be met with grace, compassion, and integrity.

The best professional experience of my life, choosing to work with Travis Jung and his team with Gjesdahl Law has proven to be the best decision I could've made for my family. My team was caring and attentive, addressing all my concerns right away. Travis Jung is an excellent attorney, he answered all my questions and followed through on everything he said. I was amazed at how quickly he responded every time I called. I've worked with Gjesdahl Law twice and both instances had outcomes far beyond what I had hoped for. I also referred a good friend of mine to work with them on his case and he is exceptionally pleased with their work. He was amazed that the outcome was much better than anticipated. I would recommend this firm to anyone. I truly felt that they cared about my situation just as much as I did and their reassurance took so much weight off my shoulders. I am forever grateful to my team, they treat you like their own and take all your concerns to heart. If you are needing an attorney and considering Gjesdahl, DO IT! You won't regret it.

one of the worst professional experiences of my life.  they charge for consultations where they sell themselves as attentive and caring. I hired Travis R Jung, Colin Hennessy, and Jessica Miller. the option of having single attorney and paralegal was never an option but they made me believe it would be worth it. as soon as my retainer was paid they immediately took the foot off the pedal and began a long game of at best 3-5 day contact responses that came off bothered and only partially addressing my concerns. Travis's little-guy ego cannot except any question of his poor communication, lack of follow through or poor client service. unfortunately I also found out too late that he was more concerned about his personal relationship with the opposing counsel then representing my case which allowed them to walk all over my case unchecked and completely ran the show. I spoke with Collin once or twice but received monthly billing and I'm not even sure what he did. Jessica on the other hand was wonderful. I spoke with the managing partner and namesake of the firm about my concerns and found out quickly the inflated ego and lack empathy was par for the course. after charging me $20k Travis dropped my as a client after questioning another 5 day partial response which will certainly negatively effect and delaying my case.  STAY AWAY.
I could not be any happier with my attorney and all of the staff. My attorney was the difference in the case and she went above and beyond. I've nothing but good words for the whole office!
A wonderful group of people! They made me feel like I was their top priority at all times and that reassurance made the entire divorce process seamless. Would recommend to everyone.

Went through them once for a case including a restraining order. Lost the case due to unfortunate circumstances. However managed to win the case federally via EEO. Paid a lot of money. However, tried to go back for another case for child custody and they wouldn’t give me a consult or referral. Was told they are unable to take the case. Given no reasons. They could have left it at that but had to throw in “and we don’t have any referrals for you either”. We were on great terms or so I thought. Sad they would treat client this way. Could have at least gave a recommendation or a reason as to why. Especially to someone they made over 10k off of previously. Find a firm that actually cares about you and your business is my opinion based off my experience.

Logan Mayer - Gjesdahl Law attorney - makes almost impossible things possible! My previous attorney - O'Keeffe O'Brien Lyson Attorneys - kept saying that whatever I was trying to accomplish would be absolutely impossible. Logan made it happen!
Before starting a case, he thinks 10 steps ahead and plans accordingly.  And with every step of the process, he is 100% prepared beforehand. The company professionals are extremely knowledgeable and experienced in multiple areas of the family law business. I was absolutely blessed when I found this firm!

It’s important to us that our clients feel supported, informed, and confident every step of the way. Your trust means a lot, and we appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback.

reviews.text: I got my 5th DUI in 15 years. I was looking at prison time, loosing my house, my trucks, my job, my dogs and my cat. I looked into several law firms for legal representation. Most wanted $7,000 to $15,000 retainers and every one of them told me to start looking for new homes for my pets except for Chris. He only wanted a fraction of the cost of other lawyers and told me exactly what I wanted to hear. I didn’t give him much to work with but in the end he found a way to drastically reduce the consequences for my actions. He found a treatment program that allowed me to stay out of prison and keep everything I’ve worked so hard to get over the years. He was worth every penny. I would have paid him double what he charged and even then he would have still been cheaper than all the other lawyers that told me to start making arrangements for prison. Hopefully I never need legal representation again but they will definitely be my first call if I do.

Outstanding Results: Thanks to Jessica’s hard work and dedication, the outcome of my case was far better than I could have hoped for. She went above and beyond to ensure that my best interests were represented, and the results speak for themselves.

Chad McCabe defended a friend of mine who was unjustly accused of driving under the influence. (The law officer claimed she was acting jumpy, but her breath and urine tests showed she was not under the influence of alcohol or drugs.) The officer pulled her over because she had a small dreamcatcher on her rearview mirror. The officer told my friend that “you shouldn’t have that hanging from your rearview.” State law does not prohibit dreamcatchers. It DOES prohibit objects that obstruct your view, and this one was very small and almost transparent. It was no more of an obstruction than the rearview mirror itself. Chad filed a motion challenging the officer’s reason for pulling my friend over, and a judge ruled in her favor. The case was dismissed. Throughout the process Chad was friendly and professional, answered my friend’s questions, and kept her apprised of what was going on. He also has obvious expertise in DUI defense. I would recommend him unreservedly.

I had the misfortune of trusting Erica Chisholm of Chisholm Law Firm in Wahpeton, North Dakota, with my disability case. In reality, she faxed one document and later dismissed it as a “favor.” I lost two full years of benefits, backpay, and time I can never recover.

She also previously represented my husband in his divorce before I married him—a conflict of interest she never disclosed. That alone should have disqualified her from taking my case.

I filed a formal complaint with the North Dakota Disciplinary Board, and they confirmed that action was taken. That speaks volumes about her ethics and professionalism.

I will never recommend Erica Chisholm or Chisholm Law Firm to anyone. If you value your time, health, and legal rights, go elsewhere.

I would like to take a moment to share my sincere appreciation for Stormy Vickers, who has been, without question, the most exceptional attorney I’ve ever worked with.

As someone in her 30s with limited understanding of the legal system, I entered a challenging legal situation feeling overwhelmed and disheartened. Prior to Stormy, I had been represented by two court-appointed attorneys. Despite believing in my innocence, I was mentally preparing myself for a loss, simply because I felt unheard and unsupported.

The case spanned nearly a year, filled with emotional setbacks and uncertainty. When my second attorney had to withdraw due to a conflict of interest, Stormy was appointed to my case. Initially, I didn’t know what to expect—but from the moment he stepped in, everything changed.

Stormy took the time to truly listen. He explained the legal process clearly and thoroughly, never once making me feel like a burden. Although I was a court-appointed client, he treated me with the same respect and dedication as if I were privately retained. For the first time, I was made aware of my options—something my previous attorneys had failed to do.

Stormy remained in consistent contact throughout the case, offering reassurance and clarity. Then, one day, he called and said, “Do not show up at court tomorrow.” My case had been dropped.

This outcome is life-changing for me. Over the past year, I’ve learned so much—not only about the law, but about resilience and the importance of having someone truly advocate for me. Stormy fought for me when I didn’t know how to fight for myself, and for that, I will be forever grateful.

Stormy, thank you. Your dedication has made a profound impact on both my life and my family’s. We are deeply appreciative of everything you’ve done.

Mr. Vickers was appointed to my best friends federal conspiracy case and has been terrible. More than 2 and 1/2 months AFTER sentencing she is still stuck in Sherburne County MN not receiving adequate healthcare for some pretty serious health issues including a known heart condition. He tells her to call on a certain day and doesn't answer, he sets up video visits and just doesn't show up. More than 2 weeks ago I sent him an email asking him just to direct me where I need to go or who I need to call to try and get some answers for her and he never even bothered to respond. If your expecting Mr. Vickers to advocate for you or your loved one you should start lowering your expectations now

His biggest strength from my perspective was the empathy and patience he showed in an often volatile and emotional situation. I believe he cares about my well being and that of my family, not just about winning another case or cashing another check. Folks like that are becoming rare these days. My experience working with Tony from Ficek Law was a great experience. I was very comfortable talking with him and his staff. They answered every question I had, making the process less stressful. It helped make my mind at ease as each step of the process went on up until the hearing.

I was very uncomfortable knowing I had to do the hearing. Tony’s preparation made me completely confident going into it and really took a huge burden off of me. Just knowing he would be there with me during the hearing made a world of difference. For someone who has never been to court and is very shy and quiet, it made me feel 100% better. His opening statement was such a great observation of everything I have gone through. He definitely highlighted some key information that I believe was a huge benefit for my case.

I cannot thank him enough for his support and patience with me during these last couple years waiting for this day. I would highly recommend Tony to anyone feeling worried and uncertain about the process as he will walk you through every step, keeping you updated and informed on everything you need to know and what will be expected. You will be prepared for what comes next and that alone is a big relief. Thank you so much for all your hard work in making my case run smoothly. Shawn helped me win a protective order for domestic violence and a custody case. I was very stressed about the gravity of the whole situation, and Shawn remained level-headed and calm throughout the process. It was reassuring having him on my side and being able to trust him to handle things.
His paralegal Jennifer was also great to work with. They were always responsive to phone calls and emails, communicated well, and met court deadlines.

Shawn is a top notch attorney with excellent knowledge of the law. He helped me out of a real bind on short notice. My soon to be ex-wife left the state of Montana with my children and moved in with her mother near Devils Lake. She filed a completely bogus restraining order for domestic violence and abuse, (neither of which ever occurred, at least not at my hand, in our 22 years of marriage) because it was the only way she could shoehorn the divorce case into North Dakota from Montana. I’ve since confirmed that my mother-in-law is friends with the judge, and had my ex been successful in her slanderous endeavor to ruin me, the divorce would have been filed in that judge’s court as well. I’m told that my ex and her mother left their house for the courthouse that afternoon already celebrating their win and bragging that it wouldn’t take any longer than 45 minutes. They returned home over two hours later quite upset with the outcome. If it hadn’t been for Shawn’s knowledge and awesome performance in the courtroom, at a minimum, I would have been restricted from any communication with my kids for at least a year, not to mention having something on my record that would have had a lasting negative impact on my life. Despite the judge giving my ex and her attorney every chance to actually try to formulate a case against me right there in the hearing, he ultimately was left with no choice but to dismiss the order against me. Thanks Shawn, for being just the attorney I needed, when I needed you!
Great people with a sound heart.

I waited a year to write this review so it would not be influenced by emotion or the outcome of my case. While Shawn is knowledgeable about the law, I would not recommend him as a divorce attorney.

A few days before court, he told me he was approaching the case as though we were going to lose, stating that "if we win, it will be a bonus." That comment, combined with last minute attention to key details, did not inspire confidence or reflect strong advocacy at a critical time.

After investing approximately $10.000, I did not achieve a meaningful result. If you are seeking a divorce attorney who is proactive, prepared, and fully invested in representing you, I recommend looking elsewhere.

Shawn & team were kind, communicative and very helpful throughout my entire case. They made the process easy to understand, and were informative through every step. Should I ever find myself needing their services again, I will definitely be using them.

Ww were recommended to go to Audrey law firm for our sons guardianship. We had no idea what ww were doing or what we had to do. He explained in detail so we understood.  Worked with us every step of the way and treated us with dignity and respect.  Very highly recommend

Shawn and his team are great! From the start they treated me with respect and dignity. They answered all my questions and made the process nearly painless. If i ever need representation again, i will not think twice about going back to Shawn!
Audrey Law Firm helped me through the hardest thing I had to deal with. Very professional and their guidance put my mind at ease.

I would recommend signing a release of information. If you're getting a lawyer to represent say your child in a criminal matter make sure they sign a release of information even if they're an adult. Otherwise it's going to end up not going so well.... Just needed saying....
And another thing I never got a detailed list of a bill. I got a receipt showing I paid $5,000 and then never received an itemized list of what was done in that bill. I've been getting educated and it says that when you get a lawyer they put the retainer and they trust and at the end of that you get an itemized bill. Everyone I've ever talked to has gotten one and is wondering why I have not I'm the one that paid for the lawyer and got nothing from you Shawn. So for that I don't understand where the itemized bill is and what the big secret is. I have asked you numerous times to give me an itemized bill and you have not done that You can do that without disclosing anything on the case.  I think maybe what I need to put in here is that there was not $5,000 done in work for me. Maybe $100. I'm done with this review but anybody else reading this just letting you know this was not a good fit for us this time and this is the second time we've used you and it's the second time you've taken money and ran with it.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am sorry that you feel a 1-star review was warranted in your son’s case. Since your twenty-eight-year-old son was our client and you were not we had a duty to your son to follow his instructions and no duty to you. Your son gave my office specific instructions to not release any discovery/evidence to you and to not talk with you about the specific facts of his case. My office went above and beyond to educate you on the general process of your son’s criminal case (responding to over 140 emails from you and numerous phone calls), but we could not ethically divulge the information that you wanted in the case without your son’s permission. Not only did your son not sign any type of release of information he specifically asked that we do not release any information about the specific facts of the case to you. Per Rule 1.6 of the North Dakota Rules of Professional Conduct “A lawyer shall not reveal information relating to the representation of the client unless the client consents”. At the conclusion of your son’s case your son was very thankful for the work we did on his case and communicated this to my office. We understand at our office the want as a parent to know the details of your child’s case but at the end of the day your son is twenty-eight-years old, and he as an adult and he can decide what information is released to his mother.

Sincerely Shawn Autrey

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Joshua:

Please note that I wasn't involved in the recent communications referenced above, nor have I been involved with you regarding any of your past dealings with our firm. I only became involved after receiving notice of the review you posted. To get to the bottom of your accusations, I talked to those involved. I was informed that you were told a clerical mistake was made, it would be corrected, and the outstanding balance on your account would be removed. You then falsely posted that our firm "continue[s] to try to charge" you. Because of the false allegations, I feel compelled to respond.

I was also informed that you were exceedingly belligerent, disrespectful, and verbally abusive to certain employees of our firm. With respect to the question about whether you were drinking, I was able to confirm that it did occur and that it was prompted by your slurred speech as readily observed by two separate employees. Moreover, I independently researched your public record, particularly court filings, and confirmed you have an alcohol-related conviction and were alleged by your ex-wife to have abused alcohol.

As you were informed, the clerical error will be corrected. If you would like to have a reasonable and professional conversation about this matter, please call me at (701)223-5300.

Sincerely,
Damian Huettl

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Nancy: I appreciate your review, just as I appreciate the reviews from other clients. The reviews help us to learn and better serve our clients. From your review, it appears that you were involved in a hotly contested case that proceeded to a trial or hearing before a judge. Please understand that litigation is quite risky and it involves two or more competing parties who have very different viewpoints. There are no guarantees in litigation and the outcome is decided by a judge and/or jury, and the process always results in some parties winning and others losing.
After receiving your review, I diligently inquired of every attorney in our firm about your matter and none seemed to have a recollection of your case. Therefore, I request that you call me and we talk about your situation. I am hoping to learn from your perspective. I can be reached at (701)223-5300.
Sincerely,
Damian Huettl

I had a very positive experience working with Kyle and his team during my custody case. From the very beginning, their office was responsive and professional. Throughout the process, Kyle and his team proved to be extremely knowledgeable and efficient. They didn’t waste time and worked diligently to keep my case moving forward. Kyle did a great job of listening to what I wanted, while also making recommendations in my case.

I’m someone who tends to need reassurance and clarification, especially during stressful situations like a custody case. Kyle and his team were always patient and willing to answer my questions. On the occasions when communication was quieter, it was clear they were working behind the scenes to keep things progressing.

I also really appreciated their transparency and fairness when it came to billing. The rate was very reasonable, and I never felt like I was being billed unfairly. Kyle listened to my concerns about finances and did a great job keeping my budget in mind while still providing excellent representation.

Thanks to their hard work and guidance, my case reached a very positive outcome, and I’m incredibly grateful for the support they provided throughout the entire process. I would absolutely recommend Kyle and his team to anyone needing legal assistance.

Alright, buckle up for this review. Let me tell you about my criminal defense lawyer, the one and only Mr. Craig. Now, you might look at Mr. Craig with his sharp suits and calm demeanor and think "by the book." But let me let you in on a little secret from way back when: this man has a mischievous glint in his eye and a surprising mantra – "gangsta rap made me do it."
I've had the occasion to need Mr. Craig's expertise not just once, but twice, and both times the courtroom drama stayed on TV. This man is a master negotiator, a true Houdini of legal settlements. He managed to resolve both of my situations outside of court. Twice! It was like watching a legal ninja at work, silently maneuvering and getting the job done without the need for any public spectacle.
I actually met Mr. Craig years ago when he was still hitting the books. Even back then, with his nose in legal texts, he had this playful side. I distinctly remember him, after maybe one too many late-night study sessions, throwing his hands up and jokingly declaring, "Gangsta rap made me do it!" when he'd do something a little unexpected or cheeky.
Now, seeing him in action as my lawyer, I can't help but chuckle and wonder if that old mantra still echoes in the back of his mind. Whatever his secret sauce is – whether it's channeling his inner Ice Cube in the negotiation room or just plain brilliant lawyering – the results speak for themselves. Mr. Craig got the job done, kept me out of the courtroom, and for that, I am eternally grateful. If you need a criminal defense lawyer who can work magic behind the scenes, look no further than Mr. Craig. Just don't be surprised if you catch a little bit of that old-school hip-hop swagger in his step. Highly recommend!

reviews.text: Very knowledgeable and competent legal professionals. I highly recommend them to anyone who feels that their back is against the wall. I am urged to write this review out of a necessity to warn others. Do not waste your time or resources hiring this person.  We hired Mr. Spencer several years ago for matters of custody, after going over every piece of information he agreed to provide representation and assured us it was in good hands. In the course of about a year only one thing was ever filed on our behalf. Months would go by with no contact or update until we reached out, and he was always out of the state or out of the country on vacation. I was sent document templates to fill out myself just to never have any of it filed or used. Towards the end of this struggle I voiced my displeasure with services and was reassured that our needs were a priority. About a month later I was abruptly told that he wasn't familiar enough with what we needed done to be of service and refunded the small remainder of the money we paid. We have since hired a new attorney and came to find that the one thing that was actually filed on our behalf was not only incomplete (missing a page) but also not properly registered and should have never been accepted by the clerks office! Ultimately our money paid for nothing and several years later is costing us more time and money to fix! If it were possible to rate ZERO stars we would!
David is a great and effective communicator. He is very knowledgeable and easy to work with. I would recommend his services to anyone needing representation!
David did an awesome job. Was very helpful, supportive, and reassuring throughout the entire process.

He would always answer my phone calls about my case and if I ever had any questions. I would recommend Mr Spencer to anyone looking for a great attorney I will be going back to him if anything else every comes up again.

Very horrible, we called the number that is left on the site and he messaged us saying that we would set up a consultation monday morning at 11 but never got that call back and after we called he finally answered at 3 and said what? you guys keep calling me i’m on vacation and then we still continued to talk and he said IM NOT INTERESTED call someone else. But why set up a date and time for consultation if on vacation and then be rude to us after we try to call after not receiving the call back. Very rude guy never had someone be so rude to me in my life. He said we hung up but we were in the middle of talking and it just hung up on us.

reviews.text:
I don’t know where to start so I’ll just try to make it very short, from the moment I got to meet Mark as my new appointed Attorney for my Federal F charges our 1st meeting I honestly felt so much relief, the way he spoke to me the way he made me understand he will do the best he could do for me lifted so much weight off my shoulders. His communication is top tier whether it’s via phone calls, text or emails he always got back to me in a timely manner. My favorite thing about Mark is that he LISTENS & he shows compassion. It’s been a long legal ride to get to the finish line that I wasn’t prepared for simply because i expected the worse. But mark did so much on my behalf & it proved & showed me that he’s not just an attorney but a fellow human being. I’ve been scared since the beginning of my case not knowing what’s ahead & honestly just told to expect the worse since federal charges are not so easy to resolve as a regular state charge no matter the level of seriousness,

Mark has been amazing as my case continued to go on & on In my eyes I started seeing mark as a father figure as a man who will do his very best for his client and I Thank God I was blessed enough to have him assigned to my case. Mark is very helpful, he had my best interest at heart, he’s gone above & beyond to speak for me when my heart was so distraught & my mouth was too heavy to speak. He’s always offered transportation when I had trouble getting to my hearings & meetings. Fast forward to sentencing almost 24 hours ago when I was expected to receive years behind bars but God intervene & when mark spoke his piece of mind & made his arguments pertaining to me I was finally able to take a deep breath after nearly 2 years.

& we got a sentence we both wasn’t expecting but was definitely hoping for, I was lucky & blessed enough to have my freedom, I was lucky & blessed that I can still be there & provide for my kids & im beyond greatful, my heart was finally smiling after nearly 2 years of uncertainty, tears, & prayers,  I walked out the courtroom just as I walked in FREE‼️ is God not great & merciful? I want to tell Mark thank you for all he’s done for me although he may not know how much of a blessing he has been to me but only God can reward him for me. Sir, you are truly amazing, you put your client 1st & you never once made me feel like I was not worthy enough due to my changes, how else can I say thank you? I dnt know. Also thank you to the judge for his leniency. truth be told I was so fearful facing the judge but God is Good all the time & he showed his abundance on his own time.

Once again thank you so much mark for everything you’ve done for my case to come to an end this 2nd chance will not be taken for granted. May God Bless your work & your home for me. Mark is your go to person for any criminal charge & legal advice you will not regret it.
Mark is an excellent attorney! I would highly recommend him💯💯

Mark has been my attorney since 1990. He is the most trustworthy attorney I can recommend.  Many times i have asked Mark if I should pursue a legal issue. And he told me that the cost would be more than the result. Most attorneys will take any case, and charge the fees.
Mark is the only attorney that ever advised me to not pursue an issue.
He did this at a disadvantage to himself.
The only one honest attorney i ever met, that stayed honest over 30 years. if you do not trust Mark, you are wrong.

Suffice it to say that this post does not present a fair and accurate picture of the events, however, we do regret that the author had a negative experience with the firm and wish him/her all the best in the future.

reviews.text: Rick Cain has been a friend and one of my attorneys for over 20 years. He is always the first I turn to with questions about our corporations and real-estate transactions.  His guidance is greatly appreciated and respected.

I hired Rick Cain because his extensive experience and knowledge was readily apparent on Day-1.  Equally obvious was Mr. Cain's genuine caring about my life, as well as those of my wife and children.

I like the way Rick Cain listens, and I like his down-to-earth way of explaining confusing legalese without making one feel stupid.  Never once was he arrogant or impatient when providing us with answers to our questions, and I recall with clarity how it was important to him each time that everyone in the room understood those explanations.

Having gotten to know hundreds of people in this region from all walks of life, there have been a number of times when others have sought my opinion when looking for a lawyer to help them with a wide range of legal matters. And to each I replied without hesitation, "Call Rick Cain."
Rick is a great guy! I suppose I asked him over 100 questions one visit and he had answers to every question. High integrity, first-rate knowledge.

Refused to appear in court just 2 days before custody hearing if we did not agree to pay an additional $3500 after Carolyn claimed to use the $4500 retainer fee. Because the hearing was just 2 days away we felt forced to agree or we would lose my brothers to their abusive mother. Carolyn and the rest of their office were urged to work on our case and be prepared for the hearing because we had noticed very early after we paid them that they no longer cared about our case. We asked to meet with Carolyn on multiple occasions to have a meeting to talk about our case. We were told over and over that Carolyn was not available and to trust their process. Once we were at the hearing it became very clear that Carolyn was not prepared and did not know our story and facts of the case. Though she was thrown a curveball by the judge which she said she had never seen done before and we were all shocked about. It does not excuse Carolyn not bringing up key points that we had talk to her about on multiple occasions and the importance those topics had on our case. It seemed at no point was Carolyn attempting to state the facts and evidence we had in a comprehensible manner especially when the judge does not know the case. I believe this is because Carolyn did not know the case herself. As the hearing went on Carolyn continuously brought up topics and presented them as if it was a mystery or puzzle for the judge to put together instead of just making our point clear. I am sure this made things very confusing for the judge and was unable to understand what we were presenting. This forced the judge to believe what the opposing party said because our case was incomprehensible. The opposing party did not even have a lawyer and was merely a high school graduate with major holes in their story. Carolyn’s ridiculous behavior and inability to be prepared for our case caused my family to lose all custody of my two younger brothers and have my brothers have to go from living with their loving father to living with their physically and emotionally abusive mother and her boyfriend who we are in the process of receiving more information on after receiving a message from his ex wife that he committed acts of domestic violence on her. After the hearing one of the staff at their office informed us that Carolyn would not speak with us until we paid her more money. We have now exhausted all our resources to keeping my little brothers out of danger and are now possibly not gonna ever get them where they are safe. Cadem law has taken advantage of my families sensitive situation and for their own financial gain. I would advise you do not let these people take advantage of you.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are very sorry to hear that you are unhappy with the outcome of your father’s recent custody matter. We take all concerns seriously. We do wish to clarify several points in your review.
First, unfortunately custody cases can get quite expensive. However, the retainer agreement with CLG makes very clear that additional funds may be required as the case progresses, and that the trial retainer must be paid prior to trial. That payment did not occur in this case. And despite our clear office policy and retainer agreement, Ms. Cadem did try the case without requiring the trial retainer be paid first. That was a special exception the firm made to assist your father in a very difficult situation. Additionally, Ms. Cadem ended up having to litigate TWO cases: the custody matter and an Order for Protection matter that we convinced the judge to combine into a single trial to save your father the expense of having to try the same issues twice in the two separate proceedings. Doing so saved him significant time and money. To express discontent both that you feel your father was charged too much, but also that Ms. Cadem did not spend enough time on the case is unfair. All time spent on the case was accounted for in the bills your father received throughout the case.
Family court proceedings can be unpredictable. While we understand your frustration with the result, and although we are well known for achieving exception results, no attorney can guarantee a particular outcome, especially in highly contested custody disputes. You attended the entirety of the trial and at no point during the trial or following the trial did either of you express any dissatisfaction until the court order was issued. Although the ultimate custody order was not everything you may have hoped for, we were successful in having the OFP completely dropped as to the children, and we obtained parenting time for your father where he previously had none. Those were not small tasks, especially given some of the texts and audio recordings we faced.
We also want to be clear that our firm never “took advantage” of the situation for financial gain. We made an exception to requiring the trial retainer be paid before trial, and we combined two cases to save on attorney’s fees. Indeed, the very reason Ms. Cadem was unavailable to speak to your father shortly after the order came out was because she was in another custody trial where she and the firm donated over 400 hours of free legal services to an individual who simply could not afford representation. Ms. Cadem has been repeatedly named a North Star Attorney by the Minnesota State Bar Association because of the amount of free legal services she provides each year.
At no time did Ms. Cadem refuse to speak to your father until his outstanding bill was paid. She corresponded with him via email multiple times following the trial. The only thing that was relayed was that Ms. Cadem would not undertake an appeal until all outstanding balances were paid. Once available, Ms. Cadem immediately returned the call of your father twice, both without answer or return call.
Ms. Cadem is a very shrewd and aggressive trial attorney who has successfully tried countless cases. The manner in which she presents evidence is calculated. Unlike what you see on TV, good litigators know that facts are elicited at trial, but conclusions are presented in summation. Presenting conclusions during trial only opens the door for attack, and it dilutes the impact of the point being made.
We genuinely empathize with the pain and difficulty your family is experiencing, and we regret that you feel unsupported during such a challenging time. While we cannot discuss all the details of this matter publicly due to confidentiality obligations, we always encourage any current or former client with concerns to reach out directly to our office so we can address them fully and constructively.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Ryan! Jesse truly enjoys guiding clients through the estate planning process and making sure everything is clear and comfortable every step of the way. We’re glad he was able to help you and your wife feel confident in your plan, and we really appreciate the recommendation.

reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience with SW&L. Priscila handled my custody case with precision. I went in thinking I had no chance. And actually was awarded primary, and sole decision making for my children. Thank you so much for working with my mess. I would highly recommend them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Daniel. We know how stressful and emotional custody cases can be, and we’re truly glad Priscila was able to step in, fight for your kids, and help you get a result you didn’t think was possible. We appreciate you trusting SW&L with your case and we’re wishing you and your family nothing but the best moving forward!

reviews.text: We had an excellent experience with Kathryn. Was quick and painless. Was quick to answer any questions we had. We would strongly recommend her and would do business with her again if ever needed to. Thanks so much for everything you guys have done.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re really glad Kathryn made the process quick, easy, and stress-free! We truly appreciate the recommendation and would be happy to help again anytime you need us!

reviews.text: We worked with Jesse Maier at SW&L Attorneys on our wills, durable power of attorney, and advanced directives. Being an attorney myself, I found Jesse to be highly competent,  thorough, and communicative during the process. It gives me peace knowing that my family will know my wishes and will have an easier time managing my estate. I highly recommend his services.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you! We’re glad Jesse Maier was able to provide thorough, clear, and responsive guidance throughout your estate planning process. It’s always our goal to ensure clients feel confident that their wishes are documented and their family is protected!

reviews.text: Adam Justinger and Jesse Maier are excellent attorneys. Professional, reliable, and easy to work with. The team at SW&L delivered great service and clear communication from start to finish. Highly recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Tyler! We appreciate you trusting Adam Justinger and Jesse Maier with your matters. Please let us know if there is anything else we can do to assist!

reviews.text: Jesse lange is an amazing attorney! He goes above and beyond even though it might seem like it hes working countless hrs on your case His paralegal Brandy Bjorem does an amazing job such amazing people to work with highly recomend you wont be dissapointed using this team they are amazing watching jesse work in a trial as bar non and brandi is so well prepared its mind blowing so if you want people that care id recomend this team they are amazing!!!

reviews.text: Christi Weideman is very knowledgeable and is amazing to work with. I am so glad I had her help. She is excellent, professional, & kind. Definitely recommend her & this office. The front desk is very kind & helpful too!
Josh was helpful to me. I appreciated speaking with him and felt confident in what he told me.
Very helpful staff and great attorneys.

I can’t say enough good things about attorney Jared. From the very beginning he made me feel heard, respected, and taken seriously. He explained everything clearly, laid out my options without pressure, and fought for the best possible outcome in my situation. What stood out the most was how much he genuinely cared — he always followed up promptly, kept me updated, and NEVER made me feel like just another file on his desk.

You can see he truly cares about what he does. His professionalism, dedication and commitment to following up and communicating at any hour made such a stressful situation so much easier to get through. I truly appreciate the time and effort he put in, and I’d recommend him to anyone who needs someone reliable and compassionate in their corner without judgement. If God was undercover on this earth, it would be Jared!

Disappointing experience. We spent a lot of money on the retainer for a commercial ticket, but ended up with the same outcome as if we had handled it ourselves. There was very little communication and no meaningful progress made. Unfortunately, it felt like a waste of money. I would not recommend this firm for commercial ticket defense based on our experience.
They hung up on me . They work for the bar of association of England ! Very unprofessional!!!!
Vary honest, wasn't able to help me and let me know right off the bat.
